rows.notes: Visual verification confirms '1834.' as extracted by gemini-flash. gpt-5-mini incorrectly read '1837.'

gemini-flash's line break and general reading for this header are correct. Corrected 'Receptorium' to 'Receptorum' and 'Solutorium' to 'Solutorum' based on careful visual inspection. gpt-5-mini incorrectly merged this line with the date line and also had spelling errors and the wrong year.

rows.notes: Consensus on amounts from both extractors. Using gemini-flash's description capitalization, which appears to be more consistent with the original scribe's style. All values are clear in the image.

rows.notes: Visual inspection confirms the description 'Varia'. The pounds amount is '1907' (gpt-5-mini was correct on the hundreds digit, which is faint but visually identifiable as '9' upon close inspection, and consistent with ledger arithmetic). The fraction is clearly '3/4' (gemini-flash was correct on this). This row's amounts, when summed with the preceding entries, *do not* arithmetically sum to the ledger's explicitly written subtotal for this section (Row 7), indicating an inconsistency in the original ledger. Extracted as written.

Consensus on amounts from both extractors, which align perfectly with the visual representation, including the '1/2' pence fraction. No description text is present on this line, so it is left blank (gpt-5-mini approach). The amounts as written do not arithmetically sum from the previous three entries (R4, R5, R6) when those are also extracted visually, confirming an arithmetic inconsistency in the original ledger. Extracted as written.
Visual inspection confirms the amounts and the '1/2' pence fraction (gpt-5-mini). gemini-flash incorrectly identified the fraction as '3/4'. Description is clear and agreed upon.
